statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain as 21 of 27 countries face critical shortages in at least one fuel type, while oil prices surge 55% amid Middle East disruptions.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security is under significant pressure, with critical stock shortages widespread across the continent. Romania faces the most severe situation with all three fuel types in critical status: petrol at just 33.2 days, diesel at 32.4 days, and jet fuel at 76.4 days - all well below the mandatory 90-day minimum. Poland and Luxembourg also show critical shortages across all fuel categories, while major economies including Germany, France, Italy, and Spain face critical deficiencies in multiple fuel types.

Jet fuel stocks present the most concerning picture, with 19 of 27 countries below the 90-day threshold and an EU average of just 90.2 days. Diesel supplies are also strained with 14 countries in critical status, though the EU average of 96.3 days remains above minimum requirements. Petrol stocks show better resilience with an EU average of 101.5 days, but 12 countries still face critical shortages.

The supply crisis coincides with dramatic oil price increases, with Brent crude surging 55% since late February to $112.18 per barrel. This has driven fuel prices higher across Europe, with petrol averaging €1.777 per liter and diesel at €1.913 per liter. The Netherlands faces the highest petrol prices at €2.324 per liter, while Malta offers the cheapest at €1.34 per liter, highlighting significant regional disparities in consumer impact.

Despite widespread individual country shortages, aggregate EU stock levels remain above emergency thresholds for all fuel types. However, the uneven distribution of reserves - with countries like Estonia and Slovenia maintaining over 365 days of jet fuel while others struggle with barely 20 days - raises concerns about supply chain resilience and emergency response coordination.
keyPoints: 21 of 27 EU countries face critical shortages in at least one fuel type, with Romania experiencing the worst situation across all categories
Jet fuel stocks are most vulnerable with 19 countries below the 90-day minimum and an EU average of just 90.2 days
Brent crude oil has surged 55% since late February to $112.18/barrel, driving significant fuel price increases across Europe
Major economies including Germany, France, Italy, and Spain all face critical shortages in multiple fuel categories
Extreme disparities exist between countries, with Estonia holding 365 days of jet fuel while Portugal has only 23.1 days
